content.problemStatement:
Given two undirected binary trees T1T_1T1‚Äã and T2T_2T2‚Äã , check if the second tree T2T_2T2‚Äã exists as a **subtree** in the first tree T1T_1T1‚Äã.

A **binary tree** is a tree in which each node has at most two children.

A **subtree** of a node xxx in a tree TTT is a tree consisting of the node xxx and all of its **descendants** in TTT. A **descendant** of a node xxx is any node reachable from it by repeated proceeding from a parent to its child.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains two space separated integers NNN and MMM ‚Äî the number of nodes in the two binary trees.
Next N‚àí1N - 1N‚àí1 lines contain three space separated characters p1i,c1i,Rip1_i, c1_i, R_ip1i‚Äã,c1i‚Äã,Ri‚Äã, describing the first tree with edge informations - p1ip1_ip1i‚Äã is an integer denoting the parent node of the iiith edge, c1ic1_ic1i‚Äã is child node, and the letter RiR_iRi‚Äã denotes whether the child is left child or right child, it's value is `L` if c1ic1_ic1i‚Äã is left child of p1ip1_ip1i‚Äã, else `R`.
Similarly, next M‚àí1M - 1M‚àí1 lines contain three space separated characters p2i,c2i,Rip2_i, c2_i, R_ip2i‚Äã,c2i‚Äã,Ri‚Äã, describing the second tree with edge informations - p2ip2_ip2i‚Äã is an integer denoting the parent node of the iiith edge, c2ic2_ic2i‚Äã is child node, and the letter RiR_iRi‚Äã denotes whether the child is left child or right child, it's value is `L` if c2ic2_ic2i‚Äã is left child of p2ip2_ip2i‚Äã, else `R`.


Output Format

Complete the given function whic should return a single integer 111 if the binary tree T2T_2T2‚Äã exists as a subtree in the binary tree T1T_1T1‚Äã and 000 otherwise.
constraints: All pip_ipi‚Äã's and cic_ici‚Äã's are distinct.
